perturbation.original_text: The Sub-Advisor shall act honestly, with due skill, care and diligence and fairly and in the best interest of the Partnership in carrying out its obligations under this Agreement and shall use all reasonable endeavours to perform its obligations under this Agreement in accordance with FSMA, the FCA Rules and any other laws, regulations, guidelines and guidance as may be in force from time to time and applicable to the Funds and their business or to the Sub-Advisor ("Applicable Law").
perturbation.changed_text: The Sub-Advisor shall act honestly, with due skill, care and diligence and fairly and in the best interest of the Partnership in carrying out its obligations under this Agreement. However, the Sub-Advisor's commitment to performing its obligations in accordance with FSMA, the FCA Rules, and other Applicable Law is subject to Oaktree US's directives, which shall take precedence in cases of conflict. The Sub-Advisor will use reasonable efforts to meet regulatory obligations, but is not liable for outcomes when following Oaktree US's instructions, even if those instructions lead to non-compliance with the Applicable Law.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that the Sub-Advisor must comply with all applicable laws. The modified version introduces a contradiction by stating that the Sub-Advisor's compliance with laws is subordinate to Oaktree US's directives, potentially leading to non-compliance. This creates uncertainty about the Sub-Advisor's responsibility for legal compliance and contradicts the original intent of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Sub-Advisor shall act honestly, with due skill, care and diligence and fairly and in the best interest of the Partnership in carrying out its obligations under this Agreement. However, the Sub-Advisor's commitment to performing its obligations in accordance with FSMA, the FCA Rules, and other Applicable Law is subject to Oaktree US's directives, which shall take precedence in cases of conflict. The Sub-Advisor will use reasonable efforts to meet regulatory obligations, but is not liable for outcomes when following Oaktree US's instructions, even if those instructions lead to non-compliance with the Applicable Law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause requires the Sub-Advisor to comply with all applicable laws. The changed text makes this compliance subordinate to Oaktree US's directives, explicitly stating that the Sub-Advisor is not liable for non-compliance resulting from following Oaktree US's instructions, even if those instructions lead to violations of Applicable Law. This directly conflicts with an obligation to follow Applicable Law, therefore causing a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the perturbation 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US will pay the Sub-Advisor such fees as may be agreed between the parties from time to time (the "Service Fee").
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US may, at its sole discretion and based on performance metrics determined solely by Oaktree US, pay the Sub-Advisor a fee which may or may not correspond to the Service Fee agreed between the parties from time to time. Payment is entirely at the discretion of Oaktree US, regardless of services rendered.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that Oaktree US 'will pay' the Service Fee, establishing an obligation. The modified version introduces the possibility that Oaktree US may, at its sole discretion, choose not to pay the Service Fee or pay a different amount, directly contradicting the original statement. This creates uncertainty as to whether the Sub-Advisor will actually receive compensation for its services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US may, at its sole discretion and based on performance metrics determined solely by Oaktree US, pay the Sub-Advisor a fee which may or may not correspond to the Service Fee agreed between the parties from time to time. Payment is entirely at the discretion of Oaktree US, regardless of services rendered.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Oaktree US "will pay" the service fee, creating an obligation. The changed text uses discretionary language ("may pay, at its sole discretion") and emphasizes that payment is entirely discretionary, regardless of services rendered. This directly contradicts the original obligation and creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting the change's impact.
